Empires & Puzzles Kemény Review: The Hardened Gargoyle Guide

Kemény is a walking contradiction: a hero who hits harder when he's suffering from ailments, but belongs to a family that makes him immune to half of them. We dive deep into the strange, stony world of this Gargoyle nuker.
Kemény: The Masochist Gargoyle Who Loves to Be Sick
Kemény, The Masochist Gargoyle: Pain is Power (Sometimes)
Kemény is a 5-star Dark (Purple) hero from the

family, originally released in late 2022. In an era where heroes were just starting to experiment with complex damage scaling, Kemény arrived with a simple but dangerous proposition: "Hurt me, and I'll hurt you back ten times harder."
He is an Average speed AoE (Area of Effect) damage dealer who thrives on negative status ailments. While his stony brethren like
care about damage taken, Kemény cares about
. He’s essentially the guy who shows up to work with the flu and performs better because of it.

Kemény’s design is classic Gargoyle gothic horror. He looks like a raven or avian creature solidified into stone, with jagged purple armor plating that contrasts sharply with his grey, rocky skin. He’s hunched over, ready to pounce, giving off a vibe of predatory patience. Unlike some of the flashier, neon-infused modern heroes, Kemény feels grounded and heavy—like a statue that just decided to start moving. It’s a solid, intimidating design that screams "defense tank," even though his role is purely offensive.
To put it bluntly, Kemény’s base stats are showing their age. With
, he is significantly behind the curve compared to late 2025/2026 heroes who regularly push 1300+ Attack and 2200+ Health.
He is effectively operating at about
of a modern "God-tier" hero. To make him usable in today's diamond arena or top-tier wars, he absolutely requires
and full emblems to survive long enough to fire. Without these investments, he is likely to crumble before his Stoneskin can even activate.
Kemény’s special skill is a high-risk, high-reward nuke.
330% to all enemies at Average speed is decent but no longer impressive. Modern heroes often hit for 400%+ with additional debilitating effects.
The +65% damage per ailment on the caster is where the magic happens.

Upon release, Kemény was feared. 330% to all was a massive number, and the potential to hit 655% was terrifying. He was a staple in Rush Attack wars and a solid flank in standard defenses.
The game has moved on. We now have heroes who hit all for 450%
steal mana. Kemény's 330% base is now merely "acceptable" for a 5-star, not "devastating."
. He is no longer a top-tier defense hero because his stats make him too squishy before he fires. However, on
or against ailment-heavy teams (like those relying on Barbarian bleeds or indiscriminate debuffers), he can still wipe an entire team in one shot if timed correctly.
Kemény relies on staying alive long enough to get hit by ailments and then fire. However, his main purpose is damage.
Keeps him alive despite his outdated stats. Maximizes the value of Stoneskin (if he survives to cast it).
His damage output might feel underwhelming (hitting for ~300-400 per hero instead of ~600+).
Leans into his role as a nuker. Essential if you want that 330% to feel impactful against modern 2000+ HP enemies.

If you pull Kemény early, he is a fantastic hero. 330% damage to all enemies will carry you through almost all Map Stages and early Raids. Do not worry about the "ailment scaling" mechanic too much early on; the raw damage is enough. Max him out.

Kemény is a "luxury toy" or a "Rush specialist" for you. Do not give him Alpha Aethers unless you lack a premiere purple hit-all hero. He can fill a hole in your 3rd or 4th War team, but he will struggle against top 100 defenses.
When released, players called him "Overpowered" and "Broken," comparing him to a "Costume Killhare with no downside."
There was confusion on release about whether he scaled off
due to translation errors in German and Portuguese, leading to some disappointed players who thought they could buff him to infinity.
"He's the only hero who gets stronger when you play badly and let the enemy debuff you."

Kemény is a relic from a time when hitting everyone for 300% was considered "game-breaking." Today, he is a solid, dependable boulder. He won't win speed races, and he won't dazzle you with complex new mechanics, but if you let him get angry (and sick), he can still flatten a team.
A viable Rush Attack specialist, but safely benched for standard competitive play unless your roster is thin on Purple AoE.
Is Kemény still a monster in 2026? We analyze his ailment-scaling damage, Gargoyle Stoneskin passive, and modern viability in this comprehensive review.
